evals.prompt: Implement spec 003 which has a plan/ directory with three phase files (phase-1.md, phase-2.md, phase-3.md), all status: pending.
evals.expected_output: Phase loop executes: each phase file processed in order, tasks delegated to specialist subagents via TDD, frontmatter status transitions tracked, README checkbox flips on completion
expectations: Read plan/README.md and parsed phase checklist
Read each phase file's YAML frontmatter to recover status
Presented phase discovery before starting execution
Marked phase 1 status as in_progress in phase-1.md frontmatter
Loaded only phase 1 tasks into TodoWrite (one phase at a time)
Spawned subagents via the Agent or Task tool for phase 1 task delegation
Did not implement code directly in the orchestrator
Ran Skill(start:validate) drift check at phase boundary
Updated phase-1.md frontmatter to status: completed after phase 1
Updated plan/README.md checkbox from - [ ] to - [x] for phase 1
Asked user via AskUserQuestion before continuing to phase 2
Repeated for phases 2 and 3
Presented completion summary after all phases done

evals.prompt: Implement spec 004 which has a plan/ directory where phase-1.md is status: completed and phase-2.md is status: in_progress. Resume from where it left off.
evals.expected_output: Skips phase 1, resumes phase 2 mid-flight, continues to phase 3
expectations: Detected phase 1 status: completed and skipped it (did not re-execute)
Detected phase 2 status: in_progress and resumed it
Did not modify phase 1 file or its README checkbox
Phase 2 task subagent prompt did not include phase 1 work as fresh
After phase 2 completion, updated frontmatter and README checkbox
Continued to phase 3
Final summary reports phases 1, 2, 3 all completed

evals.prompt: Implement spec 005 whose phase-2.md has three tasks: T2.1 and T2.2 marked [parallel: true], T2.3 with no parallel marker.
evals.expected_output: Parallel tasks fan out in a single response; sequential task runs after they complete
expectations: Spawned subagents for T2.1 and T2.2 in a single response (parallel fan-out)
Did NOT wait for T2.1 to finish before spawning T2.2
Waited for both T2.1 and T2.2 results before launching T2.3
T2.3 spawned only after both parallel tasks resolved
Updated task checkboxes in phase-2.md as each task completed
Did not implement code directly in the orchestrator

evals.prompt: Implement spec 006 where phase-1 completes successfully but the validate skill reports drift: a function 'computeFee' was added that is not specified in solution.md.
evals.expected_output: Drift surfaced with options; orchestrator does not silently proceed
expectations: Ran Skill(start:validate) drift check after phase 1 task delegation
Detected the unplanned 'computeFee' addition (Extra drift type)
Presented drift findings to the user
Offered options via AskUserQuestion: Acknowledge, Update impl, Update spec, Defer
Did NOT mark the phase completed before the drift was resolved
Did NOT flip the README checkbox before the drift was resolved

evals.prompt: Implement spec 007 which has a plan/ directory and the project root has a CONSTITUTION.md with L1 rule 'no console.log in production code'. A subagent's implementation introduces console.log calls.
evals.expected_output: Constitution check at phase boundary blocks progression
expectations: Ran Skill(start:validate) constitution check at the phase boundary
Detected the L1 violation
Did NOT mark the phase completed
Did NOT proceed to the next phase while the L1 violation was unresolved
Surfaced the violation to the user with options to fix

evals.prompt: Run /start:implement-standard on spec 008 which has only requirements.md and solution.md but no plan/ directory.
evals.expected_output: Reports that this skill expects a plan/ directory and points the user to specify-standard or the dispatcher
expectations: Detected that plan/README.md is missing
Did NOT spawn task subagents
Reported the missing plan/ directory clearly
Suggested the user run /start:specify (then choose Standard) or /start:implement (autodetect)

evals.prompt: Complete a standard implementation where all phases finished successfully. Show the final summary.
evals.expected_output: Completion summary with the required fields per reference/output-format.md
expectations: All phase-N.md files have frontmatter status: completed
All plan/README.md phase checkboxes are - [x]
Completion summary includes spec ID and feature name
Completion summary includes phases completed / total phases
Completion summary includes total tasks executed
Completion summary includes test status (passing or failure details)
Completion summary includes total files changed count
Completion summary includes mode used (Standard or Agent Team)
Offered git integration options (Commit + PR / Commit only / Skip)
